name: Salmon Steaks with Wine Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. If using frozen salmon steaks, thaw them first.
2. Preheat a 6 1/2-inch microwave browning dish on 100% power for 3 minutes.
3. Add cooking oil to the browning dish and swirl to coat the dish.
4. Place the fresh or thawed salmon steaks in the browning dish.
5. Microwave, covered, on 100% power for 30 seconds.
6. Turn the salmon steaks and microwave, covered, on 50% power for about 3 minutes, or until the salmon flakes easily when tested with a fork. Let stand, covered, while preparing the wine sauce.
7. For the wine sauce, in a 4-cup measure, micro-cook butter or margarine uncovered on 100% power for 45 seconds to 1 minute, or until melted.
8. Stir in cornstarch and white pepper.
9. Stir in half & half light cream. Microwave, uncovered, on 100% power for 2 to 3 minutes, or until mixture is thickened and bubbly, stirring every minute.
10. Stir half the hot cream mixture into the beaten egg yolk. Return all to the 4-cup measure.
11. Micro-cook, uncovered, on 50% power for 1 minute, stirring every 15 seconds.
12. Stir until mixture is smooth. Stir in dry white wine.
13. Transfer salmon steaks to a serving platter. Spoon the wine sauce atop. Garnish with seedless green grapes, if desired.
